completed_slice: Add a deny-by-default adapter that converts GitHub CLI artifact-attestation verification output into a narrowly scoped signature-verification decision.

verified_properties: signed provenance uses Sigstore-issued short-lived certificates
public repositories use the public-good Sigstore instance
verification is performed with GitHub CLI attestation verify
workflow signing requires id-token:write and attestations:write

verification.source_review: Committed module and tests were written against the official action contract.
verification.runtime_limit: No GitHub Actions run or gh attestation verify output exists for these commits yet.
verification.workflow_status: The production deployment workflow intentionally remains unwired and continues to reject unsigned evidence.

privacy_boundary: Contains only public repository identities, workflow paths, commit SHAs, and abstract verification rules; no credentials, secret values, health data, or private chat material.
claim_limits: The adapter does not itself perform cryptographic verification.
A verified signature authenticates provenance identity and subject binding; it does not prove the deployment or scientific claim is true.
C-001 remains blocked on Cloudflare credentials and an executed deployment workflow.
